steps: Viral lysates should be passed through a 0.22μm filter to remove host debris, then treated with nucleases to degrade extracapsular nucleic acids. We added 25μl DNAse I, 50μl RNAse A/T1 and 1X DNAse I Buffer to 1ml filtrate, and incubated for 1 hour 30 minutes at 37ºC.
RNA should be extracted using a commercially available kit. We used an RNeasy Mini Kit (Qiagen). Kits that require carrier RNA are not recommended because this will interfere with sequencing.
Quantify the RNA using Qubit. Note that low viral titres and/or small viral genomes may result in the amount of RNA being below the Qubit's limit of detection.

Add 50% DMSO to RNA sample, and incubate for 1 hour 30 minutes at 65ºC. Purify RNA using a column cleanup kit (we recommend Qiagen's RNeasy MinElute Cleanup Kit) or your preferred other method (e.g. ethanol precipitation, TRIzol purification).

First strand synthesis. Add 5μl purified RNA to 1μl random hexamers, 1μl dNTPs and 3μl nuclease-free water, and incubate for 5 minutes at 65ºC. Place on ice for 1 minute, then add 0.01M DTT, 5mM MgCl2, 1X reverse transcriptase buffer, 1μl RNAseOUT and 1μl Superscript III enzyme to each reaction, using nuclease-free water to bring reactions to a total volume of 20μl. Incubate in a thermal cycler for 10 minutes at 25ºC, 50 minutes at 50ºC and 5 minutes at 85ºC. Place reactions on ice.

Second strand synthesis. Add 1μl dNTPs, 0.5μl DNA ligase, 2μl DNA polymerase I, 0.5μl RNAse H, 1X second strand synthesis buffer, and use nuclease-free water to bring the total volume to 40μl. Incubate for 5 hours at 16ºC, and purify DNA using your preferred method (we used a Macharey-Nagal Nucleospin Gel and PCR Clean-up kit). Quantify DNA using Qubit.
Prepare DNA libraries for sequencing using your chosen platform and library preparation kit (we used Nextera XT).
creator: Alex Wilcox
